signals.idea: Migrate ALL task creation logic into create-task edge function: move param building, defaults, validation, resolution calc, LoRA formatting, ID generation from 6 client-side task families into server-side resolvers. Batch support inside edge function. Travel orchestrator creates only orchestrator task. No backward compat needed. Clean migration of every caller, remove all client-side param building code.

unresolved_flags.concern: Task API: Step 17 removes the raw `createTask({ project_id, task_type, params })` path before migrating all existing direct callers.
Travel contracts: Scope creep: deleting `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/` would remove shared read-side contracts and helpers, not just task creation code.
Family exports: deleting all of `src/shared/lib/tasks/families/` is incomplete because several family modules are used as shared type surfaces, not only as task builders.
Response handling: the planned batch and travel response shape changes are not wired through the existing client parsing and placeholder lifecycle.
Cleanup inventory: Step 18 proposes deleting shared client utilities that are still used outside the task-building flow.
Batch idempotency: the batch insert design does not match the current edge function's single-key duplicate-recovery logic.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 7.75. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 6.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items: TypeScript compiles with zero errors after all client-side param-building code is deleted
Every resolver produces task params byte-identical (or semantically equivalent) to the pre-migration client-side output for the same input — verified by snapshot comparison
Batch requests create exactly N task rows from a single HTTP request (verified in DB)
All 10+ task families work end-to-end: UI action → edge function resolver → correct DB row → worker processes successfully → generation appears in UI
No client-side file in `src/shared/lib/tasks/families/`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/`, or `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageEditing/` exists after cleanup
Edge function cold start time does not regress by more than 500ms (measured via Supabase logs)
Resolution calculation produces identical results server-side (verified by logging both client and server results during migration overlap)
The `create-task` edge function handles validation errors with clear 400 responses including field-level error details (matching current client-side TaskValidationError behavior)

unresolved_flags.concern: Task API: Step 17 removes the raw `createTask({ project_id, task_type, params })` path before migrating all existing direct callers.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The repo still has raw `createTask(...)` task creators outside the listed 6 families + 4 outliers: `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/modes/useVideoEditing.ts:105`, `src/tools/edit-video/hooks/useReplaceMode.ts:337`, and `src/tools/character-animate/lib/characterAnimate.ts:127`. Following the plan as written would break those paths while claiming a clean migration of every caller.

unresolved_flags.concern: Travel contracts: Scope creep: deleting `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/` would remove shared read-side contracts and helpers, not just task creation code.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/index.ts:1-31` re-exports types/defaults/read helpers like `resolvePrimaryStructureVideo`, and those are consumed across UI code such as `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/SharedGenerationView.tsx:22` and `src/shared/components/SegmentSettingsForm/segmentSettingsUtils.ts:2-6`. The directory is not creation-only, so deleting it is broader than the stated migration.

unresolved_flags.concern: Family exports: deleting all of `src/shared/lib/tasks/families/` is incomplete because several family modules are used as shared type surfaces, not only as task builders.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts:2` imports `ReferenceMode` from `@/shared/lib/tasks/families/imageGeneration` and re-exports family API types at `:136+`; `src/shared/components/modals/ImageGenerationModal.tsx:10,44-56` imports both `createBatchImageGenerationTasks` and `BatchImageGenerationTaskParams`; `src/shared/components/SegmentSettingsForm/segmentSettingsUtils.ts:6` imports `IndividualTravelSegmentParams`. The plan names a few call-site rewrites, but not relocation of these shared types/contracts.

unresolved_flags.concern: Response handling: the planned batch and travel response shape changes are not wired through the existing client parsing and placeholder lifecycle.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/parseTaskCreationResponse.ts:24-40` only accepts a single `task_id`. `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskPlaceholder.ts:33-64` only extracts IDs from `task_id` or arrays of `{ task_id }`, not `{ task_ids: string[] }`. Also `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/createTravelBetweenImagesTask.ts:94-103` currently gets `parentGenerationId` from client-side preparation, so Step 15's server-returned `parentGenerationId` needs an explicit parsing path that the plan does not cover.

unresolved_flags.concern: Cleanup inventory: Step 18 proposes deleting shared client utilities that are still used outside the task-building flow.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/resolution.ts:33` is still used by reference-processing flows in `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/referenceDomainService.ts:8,79-80` and `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useReferences.ts:10,76`. `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/ids.ts:1-19` is also used outside task creation, e.g. `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:2,137`. `src/shared/lib/tasks/segmentGenerationPersistence.ts:71-80` still exports `persistSegmentEnhancedPrompt`, which is imported by `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts:24`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Batch idempotency: the batch insert design does not match the current edge function's single-key duplicate-recovery logic.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Current duplicate recovery in `supabase/functions/create-task/index.ts:158-178` assumes one insert, one `idempotency_key`, and one `.single()` recovery query. A multi-row `.insert(array)` with per-task keys can fail the whole batch on one unique-key conflict, and the current recovery path cannot map that failure back to multiple existing task IDs. The plan says 'one idempotency key per task' but does not specify conflict handling or partial recovery semantics.
